message.content:
I'm working in a Kotlin Multiplatform repo at /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp. There's a legacy iOS app under `iosApp/RadiaCode/` (Swift) that we're porting to shared KMP code. I need to understand TWO things — be thorough but report only conclusions, not full file dumps.

1) The iOS "BluetoothDevice" / device-connection model. Search `iosApp/RadiaCode/Services/Device/` and `iosApp/RadiaCode/Services/` broadly for the Swift type(s) that represent a connected device and the connection manager/controller. I specifically want to know:
   - The name(s) and responsibilities of the connected-device class (often a god-object).
   - What properties/state it exposes (device identity, transport/peripheral, signal/RSSI, battery, device info like serial/firmware, connection state, records/data streams, configuration).
   - The connection lifecycle: how connect/disconnect/auto-reconnect is orchestrated, what states exist (e.g. an enum of connection states), and which class owns that.
   - Any "DeviceManager"/"ConnectionController"/"BluetoothManager" type and its public surface (methods + observable state).

2) Confirm what currently exists in the Android app shell. Look at `androidApp/src/main/kotlin/com/radiacode/android/`. Report whether there is ANY existing navigation, screen scaffolding, ViewModel, or DI (Koin) setup — or whether it's just the placeholder `App()` composable showing a Greeting. List the files under `ui/` and note their purpose briefly.

Report back as a concise structured summary: (A) iOS connected-device model — name, fields, responsibilities; (B) iOS connection manager — name, states, public methods; (C) Android shell — what exists today (screens/VM/nav/DI or just placeholder). Give file paths for the key Swift/Kotlin files you found.

attachment.content:
- accessorysetupkit: Discover and configure Bluetooth and Wi-Fi accessories using AccessorySetupKit. Use when presenting a privacy-preserving accessory picker, defining discovery descriptors for BLE or Wi-Fi devices, handling accessory session events, migrating from CoreBluetooth permission-based scanning, or setting up accessories without requiring broad Bluetooth permissions.
- activitykit
- adattributionkit: Measure ad effectiveness with privacy-preserving attribution using AdAttributionKit. Use when registering ad impressions, handling attribution postbacks, updating conversion values, implementing re-engagement attribution, configuring publisher or advertiser apps, or replacing SKAdNetwork with AdAttributionKit for ad measurement.

- make: Run the project Makefile (regenerates Xcode project via xcodegen and opens it).
- post-merge: Sync main after a PR has been merged (git checkout main + pull --ff-only + make).
- pr: Wrap current uncommitted changes into a Feature/RAD-XXXX branch and open a Bitbucket PR.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- pdf-viewer:annotate: Collaboratively annotate a PDF — propose markup, review together, iterate
- pdf-viewer:fill-form: Fill PDF form fields interactively with live visual feedback
- pdf-viewer:open: Open a PDF in the interactive viewer
- pdf-viewer:sign
- pdf-viewer:view-pdf
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory
- anthropic-skills:docx
- anthropic-skills:extract-design
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx
- anthropic-skills:schedule
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator
- anthropic-skills:xlsx
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.text: Now let me read the key iOS files to understand the device model and connection lifecycle.

content.content:
1	//
2	//  BluetoothDevice.swift
3	//  Radiacode
4	//
5	//  Created by Pavel Marinchenko on 14.03.2023.
6	//
7	
8	import Foundation
9	import Combine
10	import FactoryKit
11	
12	@Observable
13	final class BluetoothDevice {
14	
15	    @ObservationIgnored
16	    @Injected(\.settingComposer) private var settingComposer
17	
18	    enum BluetoothDeviceError: Error {
19	        case transmition
20	    }
21	    
22	    // MARK: Private properties
23	    @ObservationIgnored
24	    private var transmition: DeviceTransmition?
25	    
26	    @ObservationIgnored
27	    private var connectionStateSubscription: AnyCancellable?
28	    @ObservationIgnored
29	    private var bluetoothSignalSubscription: AnyCancellable?
30	    @ObservationIgnored
31	    private var settingsChangedSubscription: AnyCancellable?
32	
33	    // MARK: Properties
34	
35	    /// Устройство
36	    let radiacode: RadiaCode.Device
37	    
38	    /// Объект для взаимодействия с блютуз протоколом
39	    let peripheral: Peripheral?
40	
41	    /// Состояние устройства
42	    var state: PeripheralImpl.ConnectionState = .disconnected
43	    /// RSSI
44	    var signal: BluetoothSignal
45	    
46	    /// Настройки
47	    var settings: Settings
48	    /// Прошивка
49	    var firmware: Firmware
50	    /// Пороговые значения тревог
51	    var alarms: Alarm
52	    /// Настройки спектра
53	    var spectrumSettings: SpectrumSettings
54	    /// Настройки устройства
55	    var configurationSettings: ConfigurationSettings = .default
56	    /// Базовое установленное прибором время (timeIntervalSince1970)
57	    var deviceBaseDate: Double = 0.0
58	    
59	    init(
60	        radiacode: RadiaCode.Device,
61	        signal: BluetoothSignal,
62	        peripheral: Peripheral?
63	    ) {
64	        self.radiacode = radiacode
65	        self.signal = signal
66	        self.peripheral = peripheral
67	        
68	        self.settings = .init()
69	        self.firmware = .init()
70	        self.alarms = .init()
71	        self.spectrumSettings = .init()
72	                
73	        self.setupObservers()
74	    }
75	
76	    @discardableResult
77	    func send<T: BluetoothCommand>(command: T) async -> Result<T.Response, Error> {
78	        guard let transmition else {
79	            return .failure(BluetoothDeviceError.transmition)
80	        }
81	        return await transmition.send(command: command)
82	    }
83	    
84	    func start() async {
85	        transmition = DeviceTransmition(peripheral: self.peripheral)
86	        await setupSettingsObserver()
87	    }
88	
89	    func reset() async {
90	        connectionStateSubscription = nil
91	        bluetoothSignalSubscription = nil
92	        settingsChangedSubscription = nil
93	        await stopCommands()
94	    }
95	    
96	    func stopCommands() async {
97	        await transmition?.stop()
98	        transmition = nil
99	    }
100	    
101	    deinit {
102	        Log.debug("BluetoothDevice Removed \(identifier)")
103	    }
104	}
105	
106	// MARK: - Configuration
107	private extension BluetoothDevice {
108	
109	    func setupObservers() {
110	        connectionStateSubscription = peripheral?.connectionState
111	            .removeDuplicates()
112	            .dropFirst()
113	            .receive(on: DispatchQueue.main)
114	            .sink { [weak self] state in
115	                guard let self = self else { return }
116	                self.state = state
117	                Log.debug("\(self.identifier) STATE: \(state)")
118	            }
119	
120	        bluetoothSignalSubscription = peripheral?.bluetoothSignal
121	            .removeDuplicates()
122	            .receive(on: DispatchQueue.main)
123	            .sink { [weak self] signal in
124	                self?.signal = signal
125	            }
126	    }
127	    
128	    func setupSettingsObserver() async {
129	        settingsChangedSubscription = await settingComposer.settingsChangedSubject
130	            .sink { [weak self] settingComposer in
131	                guard let self = self else { return }
132	                Task { @MainActor in
133	                    self.settings.vibration = await settingComposer.isEnabled(setting: .device(.vibration))
134	                    self.settings.clickers = await settingComposer.isEnabled(setting: .sound(.clickers))
135	                    self.settings.sound = await settingComposer.isEnabled(setting: .device(.sound))
136	                    self.settings.leds = await settingComposer.isEnabled(setting: .device(.leds))
137	                    self.settings.power = await settingComposer.isEnabled(setting: .device(.power))
138	                    
139	                    let soundAlarm = await settingComposer.isEnabled(setting: .sound(.alarm))
140	                    let vibroAlarm = await settingComposer.isEnabled(setting: .vibro(.alarm))
141	                    self.settings.alarm = soundAlarm || vibroAlarm
142	                }
143	            }
144	    }
145	}
146	
147	extension BluetoothDevice {
148	
149	    @Observable
150	    final class Settings {
151	        var vibration = false
152	        var clickers = false
153	        var sound = false
154	        var leds = false
155	        var alarm = false
156	        var power = false
157	        var temperatureUnit: TemperatureUnit = .celsius
158	    }
159	}
160	
161	extension BluetoothDevice: Equatable {
162	
163	    static func == (lhs: BluetoothDevice, rhs: BluetoothDevice) -> Bool {
164	        lhs.radiacode == rhs.radiacode
165	    }
166	
167	    static let mock = BluetoothDevice(
168	        radiacode: .mock,
169	        signal: .strong,
170	        peripheral: nil
171	    )
172	}
173

1	//
2	//  DeviceConnectionManager.swift
3	//  Radiacode
4	//
5	//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 11.09.2023.
6	//
7	
8	import UIKit
9	import CoreBluetooth
10	import Combine
11	import FactoryKit
12	
13	extension Container {
14	    var deviceConnectionManager: Factory<DeviceConnectionManager> {
15	        self { DeviceConnectionManagerImpl() }
16	            .singleton
17	    }
18	}
19	
20	/// Состояние подключения прибора
21	typealias DeviceConnectionState = DeviceConnectionManagerImpl.ConnectionState
22	
23	/// Менеджер подключений
24	protocol DeviceConnectionManager {
25	    
26	    /// События изменения подключения
27	    var connectionSubject: CurrentValueSubject<DeviceConnectionState, Never> { get }
28	        
29	    /// Запуск подключения
30	    func startConnect()
31	    
32	    /// Подключение к устройству
33	    /// - Parameters:
34	    ///   - radiacode: устройство
35	    func connect(radiacode: RadiaCode) async
36	    
37	    /// Ручное отключение от устройства
38	    func manualDisconnect() async
39	}
40	
41	final class DeviceConnectionManagerImpl: DeviceConnectionManager {
42	    
43	    enum DeviceConnectionManagerError: Error {
44	        case timeout
45	    }
46	    
47	    /// Состояние подключения прибора
48	    enum ConnectionState: Equatable, CustomStringConvertible {
49	        /// Не проинициализированно
50	        case uninitialized
51	        /// Отсутсвует
52	        case empty
53	        /// Поиск
54	        case search(String)
55	        /// Переподключение
56	        case reconnect
57	        /// Не найден
58	        case notFound
59	        /// Подключен
60	        case connected
61	        /// Обновление прошивки
62	        case updatingFirmware
63	        /// Готов к использованию
64	        case ready(BluetoothDevice)
65	        /// Ошибка подключения
66	        case error
67	        /// Отключение от устройства и ошибка переподключения
68	        case disconnected
69	        
70	        var isConnected: Bool {
71	            switch self {
72	            case .connected, .ready, .updatingFirmware:
73	                true
74	            default:
75	                false
76	            }
77	        }
78	        
79	        var isReady: Bool {
80	            switch self {
81	            case .ready:
82	                true
83	            default:
84	                false
85	            }
86	        }
87	        
88	        var description: String {
89	            switch self {
90	            case .uninitialized:
91	                "Uninitialized"
92	            case .empty:
93	                "Empty"
94	            case .search(let device):
95	                "Search \(device)"
96	            case .reconnect:
97	                "Recconnect"
98	            case .notFound:
99	                "NotFound"
100	            case .connected:
101	                "Connected"
102	            case .updatingFirmware:
103	                "Updating"
104	            case .ready:
105	                "Ready"
106	            case .error:
107	                "Connection Error"
108	            case .disconnected:
109	                "Disconnected"
110	            }
111	        }
112	        
113	        var readyDevice: BluetoothDevice? {
114	            if case let .ready(device) = self {
115	                return device
116	            }
117	            return nil
118	        }
119	    }
120	    
121	    /// Способ подключения к устройству
122	    enum ConnectionType: Equatable {
123	        /// Автоматическое
124	        case auto
125	        /// Повторное
126	        case reconnect
127	        /// Ручное
128	        case manual
129	    }
130	    
131	    @Injected(\.deviceSetupManager) private var deviceSetupManager
132	    @Injected(\.deviceManager) private var deviceManager
133	    @Injected(\.deviceFoundManager) private var deviceFoundManager
134	    @Injected(\.bluetoothManager) private var bluetoothManager
135	    @Injected(\.analyticsEngine) private var analyticsEngine
136	    @Injected(\.deviceContainer) private var deviceContainer
137	    
138	    private let queue = DispatchQueue(label: "com.radiacode.ble.connection.timeout")
139	    private var isSearching = false
140	    
141	    let connectionSubject: CurrentValueSubject<ConnectionState, Never> = .init(.uninitialized)
142	    
143	    private var searchCancellable: AnyCancellable?
144	    
145	    private var cancellable: Set<AnyCancellable> = .init()
146	    
147	    func startConnect() {
148	        analyticsEngine.reportEvent(DeviceConnectionAnalyticsEvent.startSearching)
149	        
150	        guard let deviceId = deviceContainer.lastSavedDeviceId else {
151	            connectionSubject.send(.empty)
152	            return
153	        }
154	        
155	        cancellable.removeAll()
156	        connectionSubject.send(.search(deviceId))
157	        
158	        searchCancellable = deviceFoundManager.foundDeviceObserver
159	            .first(where: { $0.device.identifier == deviceId })
160	            .timeout(60 * 60, scheduler: queue, customError: { DeviceConnectionManagerError.timeout })
161	            .sink(
162	                receiveCompletion: { result in
163	                    switch result {
164	                    case .failure:
165	                        self.stopSearching()
166	                        self.connectionSubject.send(.notFound)
167	                    case .finished:
168	                        break
169	                    }
170	                }
171	                , receiveValue: { radiaCodeDevice in
172	                    self.searchCancellable = nil
173	                    self.stopSearching()
174	                    Task {
175	                        await self.connect(radiacode: radiaCodeDevice)
176	                    }
177	                }
178	            )
179	        isSearching = true
180	        deviceFoundManager.startSearching()
181	    }
182	    
183	    func connect(radiacode: RadiaCode) async {
184	        /// Отключение от предыдущего устройства
185	        await disconnect()
186	        
187	        let bluetoothDevice = BluetoothDevice(
188	            radiacode: radiacode.device,
189	            signal: radiacode.signal,
190	            peripheral: PeripheralImpl(cbPeripheral: radiacode.connection)
191	        )
192	        
193	        bluetoothDevice.peripheral?.connectionState
194	            .removeDuplicates()
195	            .dropFirst(2) /// Игнорируем изначальные состояния Disconnected и Connecting
196	            .sink { [weak self] state in
197	                guard let self = self else { return }
198	                self.analyticsEngine.reportEvent(
199	                    DeviceConnectionAnalyticsEvent.deviceConnection(identifier: bluetoothDevice.identifier, state: state)
200	                )
201	                switch state {
202	                case .connected:
203	                    self.connectionSubject.send(.connected)
204	                case .disconnected:
205	                    self.deviceManager.cancelRealTimeData()
206	                    Task {
207	                        await bluetoothDevice.stopCommands()
208	                    }
209	                    self.connectionSubject.send(.reconnect)
210	                    self.bluetoothManager.setupReconnection(device: radiacode.connection)
211	                case .connecting:
212	                    // Автоматическое переподключение
213	                    self.deviceSetupManager.setupInitialConnectionObserver()
214	                    self.startReconnectTimeout()
215	                case .disconnecting:
216	                    break
217	                }
218	            }
219	            .store(in: &cancellable)
220	        
221	        deviceSetupManager.connectedDeviceSubject
222	            .receive(on: DispatchQueue.main)
223	            .sink { [weak self] state in
224	                guard let self else { return }
225	                self.cancelReconnectTimeout()
226	                switch state {
227	                case .updating:
228	                    self.connectionSubject.send(.updatingFirmware)
229	                case .connected:
230	                    self.connectionSubject.send(.ready(bluetoothDevice))
231	                case .error:
232	                    self.connectionSubject.send(.error)
233	                }
234	            }
235	            .store(in: &cancellable)
236	        
237	        /// Сохранение устройства
238	        deviceContainer.lastSavedDeviceId = bluetoothDevice.identifier
239	        /// Сохранение устройства в менеджер для доступа к подключенному устройству и его первоначальной инициализации
240	        deviceSetupManager.setup(device: bluetoothDevice)
241	        
242	        /// Подключение к устройству по блютуз
243	        bluetoothManager.connect(device: radiacode.connection)
244	    }
245	    
246	    func manualDisconnect() async {
247	        connectionSubject.send(.empty)
248	        await disconnect()
249	    }
250	    
251	    /// Завершение автопоиска и подключения при необходимости
252	    private func stopSearching() {
253	        if isSearching {
254	            isSearching = false
255	            deviceFoundManager.stopSearching()
256	        }
257	    }
258	    
259	    private func disconnect() async {
260	        // Отменяем таймер
261	        cancelReconnectTimeout()
262	        
263	        // Удаляем подписки
264	        cancellable.removeAll()
265	        
266	        // Останавливаем поиск
267	        stopSearching()
268	        
269	        if let device = deviceContainer.current, let peripheral = device.peripheral?.cbPeripheral {
270	            // Удаляем устройство из базы для автоподключения
271	            deviceContainer.lastSavedDeviceId = nil
272	            if device.state != .disconnected {
273	                try? await bluetoothManager.disconnect(device: peripheral)
274	            }
275	        }
276	        await deviceSetupManager.reset()
277	    }
278	    
279	    private var reconnectTimeoutTask: Task<Void, Never>?
280	    
281	    private func startReconnectTimeout() {
282	        reconnectTimeoutTask?.cancel()
283	        reconnectTimeoutTask = Task {
284	            try? await Task.sleep(seconds: 60 * 10)
285	            guard !Task.isCancelled else { return }
286	            self.connectionSubject.send(.disconnected)
287	        }
288	    }
289	    
290	    private func cancelReconnectTimeout() {
291	        reconnectTimeoutTask?.cancel()
292	        reconnectTimeoutTask = nil
293	    }
294	}
295

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the structured summary.

## Summary

### (A) iOS Connected-Device Model

**Primary Class: `BluetoothDevice`**  
Path: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode/Services/Device/Model/BluetoothDevice.swift`

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Represents a **single connected Bluetooth device** (god-object pattern)
- Wraps device identity, peripheral transport, and command transmission
- Exposes observable connection state, signal strength, and device configuration
- Manages lifecycle (start, reset, stopCommands)

**Core Properties:**
- `radiacode: RadiaCode.Device` — device identity (identifier, model)
- `peripheral: Peripheral?` — BLE transport layer (CBPeripheral wrapper)
- `state: PeripheralImpl.ConnectionState` — connection state (.disconnected, .connecting, .connected, .disconnecting)
- `signal: BluetoothSignal` — RSSI strength indicator
- `firmware: Firmware` — version and feature level
- `settings: Settings` — toggles (vibration, clickers, sound, leds, power, alarm, temperatureUnit)
- `alarms: Alarm` — threshold levels (countRate, doseRate, dose)
- `spectrumSettings: SpectrumSettings` — energy calibration coefficients
- `configurationSettings: ConfigurationSettings` — device capabilities (disableSpectrum, disableHardness, maxCountRate, maxDoseRate, maxDose)
- `deviceBaseDate: Double` — device's base time (epoch)

**Command Interface:**
- `send<T: BluetoothCommand>(command: T) async -> Result<T.Response, Error>` — sends commands and decodes responses via internal `DeviceTransmition` actor

**Observable State:**
Uses Combine subscriptions (private) to forward:
- Connection state changes from `peripheral.connectionState`
- RSSI updates from `peripheral.bluetoothSignal`
- Settings changes from `settingComposer.settingsChangedSubject`

---

### (B) iOS Connection Manager

**Primary Architect: `DeviceConnectionManagerImpl`**  
Path: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode/Services/Device/DeviceConnectionManager/DeviceConnectionManager.swift`

**Responsibilities:**
- Orchestrates the **full lifecycle** of device discovery, connection, firmware update, and readiness
- Manages auto-reconnection logic with 10-minute timeout
- Publishes connection state changes to the rest of the app

**Connection State Enum:**
```
case uninitialized        // Boot state
case empty                // No saved device
case search(String)       // Searching for device by ID
case reconnect            // Attempting auto-reconnect
case notFound             // Search timeout (1 hour)
case connected            // Peripheral connected, initializing
case updatingFirmware     // FW update in progress
case ready(BluetoothDevice)  // Fully initialized, ready to use
case error                // Connection or init failed
case disconnected         // Lost after connected state; reconnect aborted
```

**Connection Lifecycle:**
1. **startConnect()** — auto-searches for last-saved device ID; publishes .search state
2. **connect(radiacode: RadiaCode)** — initiates BLE connection:
   - Creates `BluetoothDevice` wrapping the `CBPeripheral`
   - Subscribes to peripheral state changes (.connecting → .connected → .disconnected) and analytics
   - On .connected: publishes .connected state
   - On .disconnecting: calls `bluetoothManager.setupReconnection()` and publishes .reconnect
   - Delegates device setup to `DeviceSetupManager`
3. **Device Setup (via DeviceSetupManager):**
   - Waits for peripheral to reach .ready state
   - Sends initialization commands (SetExchangeCommand, configuration read, firmware fetch, calibration read, SetLocalTimeCommand)
   - On success: publishes .ready(bluetoothDevice)
   - On error: publishes .error
4. **Auto-Reconnect:** 10-minute timeout task; if not reconnected, publishes .disconnected
5. **manualDisconnect()** — user-initiated disconnect; publishes .empty and cleans up

**Public API:**
- `connectionSubject: CurrentValueSubject<ConnectionState, Never>` — observable state
- `startConnect()` — begin auto-search
- `connect(radiacode:)` — manual connection
- `manualDisconnect()` — user disconnect

**Supporting Manager: `DeviceManagerImpl`**  
Path: `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode/Services/Device/DeviceManager/DeviceManager.swift`

Roles:
- Holds reference to currently connected `BluetoothDevice`
- Implements `DeviceManager` protocol (send command, start/cancel real-time data)
- Implements `DeviceSetupManager` protocol (initial setup, settings updates, calibration)
- Fetches device settings, firmware info, and energy calibration on connection

---

### (C) Android Shell — Current State

**Status: Minimal scaffold, no navigation/DI/ViewModel infrastructure**

**Core Files:**
- `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/androidApp/src/main/kotlin/com/radiacode/android/App.kt`  
  → Placeholder composable: shows "Radiacode" title + `Greeting().greet()` text

- `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/androidApp/src/main/kotlin/com/radiacode/android/MainActivity.kt`  
  → Simple ComponentActivity; calls `setContent { AppTheme { App() } }`; enables edge-to-edge with transparent system bars

**UI Components (isolated, no ViewModel integration):**
- `BatteryLevel.kt` — reusable UI component displaying battery % with icons; supports charging state
- `BluetoothStrength.kt` — reusable UI component displaying BLE signal strength using `BluetoothSignal` enum from shared code
- `Theme.kt`, `Colors.kt`, `Typography.kt` — Material 3 theme definitions

**What's Missing:**
- No navigation framework (Jetpack Navigation, Voyager, or similar)
- No ViewModel or screen state management (Compose state, MVI, etc.)
- No Dependency Injection (Koin, Hilt, or manual factories)
- No screens or features implemented (device list, connection, data display, settings)
- UI components exist but are isolated from business logic

**Shared Code Available:**
- The Android app can reference shared KMP models (e.g., `BluetoothSignal` from `com.radiacode.shared.core.device.model`)
- No platform-specific device/connection logic implemented yet
